title: dude tell me what new features i can add understand the code first

messages.content: dude tell me what new features i can add understand the code first
ok tell me what are the new features

Looking at Evelina's architecture and current feature set, here are concrete new features you could add:

## AI & Chat Enhancements
- **Conversation branching** - Let users fork chat sessions to explore different paths without losing the original thread
- **Chat search & filtering** - Full-text search across all chat history with filters by date, model, persona, or tags
- **Export conversations** - PDF, Markdown, or HTML export with syntax highlighting and images
- **Chat templates** - Pre-defined prompt templates for common tasks (debugging, writing, analysis)
- **Voice input/output** - Speech-to-text and text-to-speech integration
- **Multi-modal uploads** - Support for PDFs, docs, and direct image analysis beyond basic screenshots

## Automation & Workflow
- **Visual automation builder** - Drag-and-drop interface for creating automations instead of JSON editing
- **Conditional workflows** - "If this, then that" logic with branching paths based on API responses or file changes
- **Webhook receiver** - Let external services trigger Evelina automations
- **Automation library** - Shareable community automations for common tasks
- **Desktop notifications** - Native OS notifications for automation triggers/completions
- **Process monitoring** - Trigger automations when specific files change or processes start/stop

## Developer Features
- **Built-in code editor** - Split-pane view for editing files while chatting with AI about them
- **Git integration** - Commit, push, and branch management directly from chat
- **API testing workspace** - Send requests, save collections, and analyze responses
- **Database browser** - Connect to and query local/remote databases from chat
- **Terminal multiplexing** - Run multiple shell commands in persistent sessions
- **Docker integration** - Manage containers and view logs from Evelina

## Integration & Data
- **Calendar integration** - Connect Google Calendar, Outlook to check schedules and create events
- **Slack/Discord bridge** - Send/receive messages from chat platforms
- **Notion/Obsidian sync** - Export chat insights to your knowledge base
- **Browser bookmarks import** - Access and search your browser bookmarks from chat
- **RSS feed reader** - Monitor and summarize content from feeds
- **Local file indexing** - Full-text search across your local documents and code

## AI Model Management
- **Model comparison mode** - Send the same prompt to multiple models simultaneously to compare responses
- **Custom model endpoints** - Add self-hosted models (Ollama, local LLMs)
- **Fine-tuning dashboard** - Track and manage fine-tuned models
- **Prompt history/versioning** - Save and iterate on effective prompts
- **Token usage alerts** - Set budgets per model/provider with warnings

## UI/UX Improvements
- **Command palette** - Quick command interface (like VS Code's Ctrl+P)
- **Custom themes** - Full theme customization or community theme support
- **Floating chat widget** - Minimize to a floating bubble while working in other apps
- **Split-screen views** - Chat + Skills, or Chat + Usage side-by-side
- **Keyboard shortcuts** - Full keyboard navigation

The modular architecture (IPC handlers, Services, Connectors) makes any of these straightforward to implement. The most impactful quick wins would be **conversation search**, **chat export**, and a **visual automation builder** - they leverage existing systems without major architectural changes.
